title: Opening protection scope after an MSFH report
description: Use this route when the report points toward windows, shutters, doors, or garage-door scope and you need to know what should be priced first.
recommendationMeaning: Opening protection usually means the report found exposed openings that still need qualifying protection. The right first move is to understand exactly which openings matter before a contractor expands the scope.
rightFirstProjectSignals: The report makes opening protection explicit.
The home is attached and current scope rules likely narrow to opening protection.
You need the cleanest quote path with the least risk of non-eligible add-ons.
qualifyingScope: Windows, shutters, exterior doors, and garage doors may need different proof and pricing.
Not every opening is necessarily in scope just because the category appears in the report.
The quote should mirror the actual recommendation instead of bundling unrelated work.
attachedHomeCaveat: If the home is attached or treated like a townhouse, opening protection may be the only grant-aligned path even when roof questions feel urgent.
confusionOrDenialRisks: Assuming every opening must be replaced when the report is narrower.
Using a quote that does not break out the actual protected openings.
Treating generic impact-window marketing as a substitute for report-based scope.

secondaryCta.label: Check contractor fit before pricing openings

relatedLinks.label: What the program may pay for
